hypothesis: Compaction work distribution obeys Jarzynski equality: J = <e^(-W/T)> = e^(-ΔF/T) ≈ 1.0 if ΔF≈0

work_distribution.verdict: consistent with both normal and exponential (underpowered n=9)

temperature_sensitivity.verdict: No T produces J≈1; ΔF varies 2x across parameterizations

verdict_detail: Thermodynamic analogy holds structurally: second law <W>>=ΔF universal, efficiency 60-82%. But Jarzynski equality FAILS quantitatively: J=0.097 (CI excludes 1.0), ΔF is path-dependent (2.58x ratio small/large). Compaction is irreversible-thermodynamic-LIKE but lacks well-defined free energy landscape. Key difference: knowledge system has no fixed equilibrium state — the compressed target evolves with system growth.
cross_domain_isomorphism: Non-equilibrium thermodynamics of irreversible compression. The 60% efficiency and path-dependent ΔF resemble driven systems far from equilibrium (Crooks 1999 regime, not Jarzynski near-equilibrium). Suggests compaction is fundamentally dissipative — 40% overhead may be structural minimum for knowledge compression in growing systems.
next: F-SP6 successor: (1) test Crooks fluctuation theorem if reverse-compaction events observable; (2) measure whether efficiency improves with session maturity (S335+ era); (3) extend proxy-K log to n>20 events for statistical power
